mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger equity sell-offs over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 24-month backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving sustained positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase indicates capacity utilization approaching limits, prompting forward-looking equity appreciation before inflationary pressures force policy tightening.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth yields negative 20-day forward returns in over 50% of late-cycle expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity that consistently supports positive equity market momentum over the following week.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.01 across multiple rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of high-inflation trigger months over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth and retail sales expansion confirm synchronized demand strength, generating positive equity returns over the subsequent month as corporate earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 60% of aligned quarterly and monthly data releases.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates directly increase corporate financing costs and compress equity valuations, producing negative forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rate hikes during tightening cycles yield positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of historical instances.
